stories.title: AWS says AI agents need business context and guardrails, and launched tools to add both
stories.summary: At its New York summit, AWS launched Context, a knowledge graph that feeds an agent the business information it needs to answer well, and a security agent that starts in a supervised mode and earns the right to act on its own only as you grant it. The wider point for any firm turning on agents is that an agent is only as useful as the context you give it and only as safe as the limits you set, and most have neither by default. Before letting an agent near real work, decide what it can see and what it is allowed to finish without a person signing off.

stories.title: A study of 500,000 grades suggests AI is lifting output, not skill
stories.summary: A UC Berkeley analysis of more than 500,000 university grades found marks rose sharply in writing and coding courses after ChatGPT, with the jump concentrated in take-home work rather than exams, a sign students used AI to produce the work rather than to learn it. The same gap can open in a firm, where AI makes the output look better while the underlying skill quietly stops growing. When your team leans on AI, check whether they are learning the work or only producing it, because that difference shows up later in who can actually judge the result.
